content: series_name is the canonical dimension for named MKC lines such as Blood Brothers, Traditions, VIP, and Ultra. If the user says line, group, run, or series and names Blood Brothers or Traditions, prefer series_name unless they explicitly ask for family or type.

family_name is the canonical dimension for a knife family such as Speedgoat, Blackfoot, Mini Speedgoat, or Super Cub. If the user says family or phrases like 'in the X family', prefer family_name. Family is different from series and type.
knife_type is the canonical dimension for broad classes like Hunting or Tactical. If the user says type, class, category, or asks for hunting knives versus tactical knives, prefer knife_type.
form_name is the canonical dimension for form-related grouping if the user explicitly asks by form. Use when the user says form, blade form, or shape in the reporting sense.

collaborator_name is the canonical dimension for collaboration relationships. If the user says collab, collaborator, or asks about Blood Brothers as a collaborator context, use collaborator_name only when the business meaning is collaboration rather than a series line.
steel is the canonical dimension for blade steel. Use when the user asks by steel, about Magnacut, or wants steel breakdowns.
location is the canonical dimension for storage or physical location. If the user says safe 1, drawer, case, or where the knives are stored, use location.

knife_name is the inventory-side item name. Use for listing owned items or exclusions against owned inventory rows. Prefer this over official_name when the question is explicitly about what the user owns.
official_name is the catalog-side model name. Use when the question is about the full catalog, missing models, or official product naming rather than owned inventory rows.

purchase_price is the per-row amount paid for an owned knife. total_spend is derived from purchase_price times quantity. If the user asks 'what did I spend' or 'what did I pay', purchase_price feeds the spend metric.
msrp is the manufacturer's suggested retail price. Use for completion-cost style questions or when the user explicitly asks about MSRP rather than purchase price.
acquired_date is the canonical inventory date field used for date filters, year buckets, month buckets, and year-over-year comparisons based on ownership acquisition.

blade_finish is the canonical field for the surface treatment applied to the blade. Use when the user asks about stonewash, satin, PVD, or other finishes. Can be used as a filter or group_by dimension.

blade_color is the canonical field for the blade color. Use when the user asks about black, bronze, sniper grey, or other blade color values. Available in inventory reports (derived from colorway data).

handle_color is the canonical field for the handle color. Use when the user asks about handle color, OD green, FDE, black, or other handle color values. Available in inventory reports (derived from colorway data). Can be used as a filter or group_by dimension.
handle_type is the canonical field for handle material or construction type. Use when the user asks about G10, Micarta, Carbon Fiber, or other handle material values.
blade_length is the canonical field for the physical blade length in inches. Use for numeric comparisons or listing blade length. Supports >, >=, <, <=, between operators.
quantity is the number of units owned for an inventory row. Use when the user asks how many of a specific knife they own, or when aggregating total counts using quantity rather than row count.
text_search is a fallback search field used only when the user is clearly searching text and no specific semantic dimension can be safely selected. Prefer a real dimension when possible.
